title: Bridge Essay: When Broca goes uninformed: reduced information flow to Broca's area in schizoph

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**When Broca goes uninformed: reduced information flow to Broca'sarea in schizophrenia patients with auditory hallucinations.** — Schizophrenia bulletin (2013). PMID: 23070537. DOI: 10.1093/schbul/sbs107. Score: 40. Auditory-verbal hallucinations (AVHs) are frequently associated with activation of the left superior temporal gyrus (including Wernicke'sarea), left inferior frontal gyrus (including Broca'sarea), and the right hemisphere homologs of both areas. It has been hypothesized that disconnectivity of both interhemispheric transfer and frontal and temporal areas may underlie hallucinations in schizophrenia.
